exercises.description: Order the sentences (B-G) below to make a story. {v} The first sentence of the story (A) is given for you as an example. {v} A.-Vogue is an American lifestyle and fashion magazine.
exercises.label: Reading Part 2

questions.title: F It started as a weekly newspaper in 1892.
G The magazine was founded by Arthur Baldwin Turnure, an American businessman.
B The first Vogue issue was published on December 17, 1892. It was priced at 10 cents in US currency.
E The largest magazine was published in September 2012; it had 916 pages.
C Vogue became a monthly publication in 1973.
D The first coloured photograph placed on a Vogue cover was in July 1932.

questions.title: B Arrive at the campsites.

E After you finished camping you must clean the place where the tent was.
F You can then start packing the essential things for your trip.

exercises.heading: TV and Films
exercises.description: Person A {v} It’s amazing when a film can make my heart beat quickly. That’s why, for me, the best film makers out there are the ones who produce horror and thriller movies. You have to be a cinematic genius to get the angles and timing perfect enough for your audience to feel the suspense and pure terror as if it were happening in real life. Don’t even get me started about how music makes these types of movies a lot better. {v} Person B {v} People call me weird, but when you ask me what type of films I like my honest answer is horror and romance. Even better is when they’re mixed together in a way that’s not too silly. I love the excitement that horror movies bring even though it is terrifying, but there’s something really real when you watch a love story that touches your heart. My dream film is one that creates a perfect marriage between the two genres. {v} Person C {v} The first time I saw a horror movie, I ended up crying and not being able to take my eyes off the screen. Even though nobody believed me, I had a great time and didn’t mind the fact that my heart was beating faster than ever before. To me, what defines a good horror movie is one that, if you turned the sound off, would still be just as amazing as before. It’s all about the suspense, and you don’t necessarily need a soundtrack to create that. {v} Person D {v} I don’t have any interest in watching scary movies because I love romance. I prefer a tragic romance story, even if it has an extremely sad ending with death. I don’t think anyone could make me watch a movie that makes me scared. Scary dreams aren’t something I want to have while I sleep, which is one of the main reasons that I don’t like to watch anything too scary.
exercises.subtitle: Four people respond to an online article about TV and Films. Read their comments and answer the questions.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks that mixing two types of films is a good idea?
2. Who thinks that the music in a film is not necessary?
3. Who gets very scared every time they watch a horror film?
4. Who thinks that horror films are not appealing?
5. Who thinks that nightmares are a result of scary films?
6. Who implies that their favourite type of film is a horror film?
7. Who thinks that a soundtrack is the most important part of a film?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: G. How to understand where you came from
C. How to teach the elderly important lessons
questions.title: 19. The presence of grandparents in a child’s life brings aspects that are unlike the {v} experiences provided by parents. Grandparents have a vast knowledge about life from {v} another generation and also know the family’s personal history. They have lived a {v} very different life and have seen a lot of changes. By simply talking to grandparents, a {v} child is able to gather a lot of information about the history and past of his or her own {v} family tree. A child is able to understand from where he or she has come and what he {v} or she belongs to.

answers: D. Learning from grandparents or learning from books
questions.title: 20. Compare learning in a classroom setting to learning about historical events from a {v} grandparent who actually lived through such moments. This is history coming from the {v} mouth of someone you know and love, someone whose word you value. Sure, {v} textbooks have their importance, but they’re also written by authors that may or may not {v} have all the same information your grandparent, who experienced these events or {v} lifestyles firsthand, has access to. Altogether, interactions like these, where knowledge {v} is passed down from one generation to another, is simply more interesting than {v} learning from a book at school. The personal touch makes all the difference.

answers: B. We learn from the past in order to shape the future
questions.title: 21. Grandparents or other older relatives have experienced things that the new generation {v} has not. Grandparents who have been present during World War II have terrifying {v} tales to tell. Such stories might be difficult to hear, as they involve much sacrifice and {v} grief, but listen closely and they’ll also give hope, strength, and more importantly, teach {v} you important lessons about how you ought to live your life. By knowing what the {v} past was all about, you have the resources you need to shape your future.

answers: E. Learn from the elderly, avoid repeating past mistakes
questions.title: 22. By listening to their stories, you come to a new appreciation for your grandparents, {v} seeing that they are not just simple old people living in the house. They have lived their {v} lives and have gone through all sorts of adventurous ups and downs. The future is {v} more or less built upon the remains of the past, and older generations hold the key to {v} truly understanding this past. When they day comes that you reach the age of your {v} grandparents, you too will have much to share with younger generations. It’s important {v} not to forget where we come from. In doing so it’s easy to fall back into old patterns {v} that cause us harm. History repeats itself, as they say.

answers: H. Stories of the past influence present viewpoints
questions.title: 23. Stories of how life was lived in the past help to shape a child’s thoughts and ideas in life. {v} For example, those who have lived through economic hardship, times of poverty and {v} having to go without many material goods, can teach today’s generation about what it {v} means to be content with what you have, even if it may not appear to be much. When {v} you imagine the poverty, or other difficulties, your grandparents endured, it gives you {v} a sense of appreciation for the comforts that you are enjoying today. The luxuries of {v} life are not the only thing that make one happy. This change of perspective is {v} something that can help bring your family closer together.

answers: F. Grandparents pass down personal values
questions.title: 24. Grandparents can shape the life of a child in more ways than one. They not only tell {v} stories about their life, but also the life of the child’s parents when they were kids. {v} Grandparents constantly try to teach personal values to the children and keep them {v} away from the attractions of modern life. They inform children about the things that are {v} important in life, such as love and family. Also, children are made aware of the {v} importance of hard work and honesty. Children who are close to their grandparents {v} grow up to become respectable citizens and respect everyone.

answers: A. Grandparents leave behind items of sentiment
questions.title: 25.Grandparents save a lot of things for their grandchildren. Some of the things being {v} property, jewelry, and so on. However, there are also items of sentimental value that {v} they save in order to pass on important information about themselves and their family. {v} Items like old documents that are proof of what life was like before you were born, the {v} marriage certificate of your parents, and more. It may even be an old family recipe {v} that you can use when you grow up. These items will always help you to remember your {v} roots and work hard to uphold the values taught to you. Whenever you feel confused {v} in life, you can turn to these things to comfort and guide you.

exercises.title: 15. Listen to a woman talk to her friend about her thoughts on standardized tests. Read the opinions below and decide whose opinion matches the statements made by the man, the woman or both the man and the woman. {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What is her opinion on living in a city? {x}
answers: There is access to many more facilities for those who live in cities.
There is too much traffic and noise to be comfortable in a city.
There is difficulty in meeting new people while living in a city.

questions.title: Question 2: What is her opinion on living in the countryside?{x}
answers: It is difficult to find transportation if you need to travel to another location.
It is healthier to live in the countryside due to lack of pollution.
It is easier to make new friends while living in the countryside.

exercises.title: 16. Listen to an agent explain the differences of living in a city to living in the countryside and answer the questions below. {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What does she think about animal cruelty in animal testing? {x}
answers: While it may be harsh on the animals, it provides invaluable advances for mankind.
It causes unnecessarily painful harm to animals and therefore alternatives should be found.
Some tests do not need to be made on animals so scientists should do their best to eliminate animal testing where possible.

questions.title: Question 2: What does she think about alternatives to animal testing? {x}
answers: Alternatives, like vitro testing, are not proven to be as useful or accurate.
There is not any obvious alternative to animal testing at this point in time.
Animal testing should be put on hold until a viable alternative has been found.

You received this e-mail from the club. {v} Dear member, {v} We are happy to announce that we can offer you a Volunteer Abroad Programme. We are searching for enthusiastic and hard-working people who have a good level of English and ready to work in a Wildlife Care Centre in South Africa. The centre is playing an important role in helping sick and injured animals. If you are interested, please contact the secretary of the club providing information on your qualifications, personal qualities and the reasons why you would like to volunteer.{v} Best wishes, {v}

title: In the Mix
description: Talk about music, describe your daily rhythm, and handle everyday situations with the confidence of a headliner.
